title: Devon Energy and Coterra Energy Complete Merger
author: Na
description: Devon Energy and Coterra Energy have successfully completed their all-stock merger, creating a premier large-cap shale operator. Under the merger agreement, each Coterra share converts to 0.70 Devon shares. The combined company will operate as Devon Energy, headquartered in Houston, with an expected $1 billion in annual pre-tax synergies by year-end 2027. Devon shareholders own approximately 54% and former Coterra shareholders own approximately 46% of the combined entity on a fully diluted basis.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The merger completion is presented as transformative and strategically beneficial, creating a premier operator with scale, inventory depth, financial strength, and $1 billion in identified synergies. Leadership expresses confidence in delivering resilient free cash flow and shareholder returns.

Coterra's world-class assets, technical capabilities, and people are highlighted as strengthening the combined entity. The merger is described as creating a company greater than the sum of its parts, with former Coterra shareholders retaining approximately 46% ownership on a fully diluted basis.

title: Devon Energy and Coterra Energy Shareholders Approve Merger
author: Globe Newswire
description: Devon Energy and Coterra Energy shareholders have approved their all-stock merger with overwhelming support. Devon shareholders voted 98% in favor while Coterra shareholders voted 99% in favor. The merger is expected to close on or around May 7, 2026, with Coterra shareholders receiving 0.70 Devon shares per Coterra share. The combined company will create a premier large-cap shale operator with enhanced margins and greater scale.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong shareholder approval (98%) for the merger, positioning Devon as the acquiring company with 54% ownership of the combined entity. The merger is expected to create a premier large-cap operator with enhanced margins, greater scale, and accelerated free cash flow growth.

Overwhelming shareholder approval (99%) for the merger. While Coterra is the acquired company, the combination is described as creating meaningful synergies and sustainable long-term value creation for all shareholders, with Coterra shareholders owning approximately 46% of the combined company.

title: Venture Global, Alcoa, And LyondellBasell Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (April 13-April 17): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Large-cap stocks experienced broad selling pressure during the week of April 13-17, 2026, with ten major companies declining significantly. Venture Global fell 14.09% following a JP Morgan downgrade, Alcoa dropped 10.23% on weak Q1 results and asset sale news, and LyondellBasell slumped 13.93%. Other notable decliners included Dow Inc. (11.08%), Equinor (10.86%), APA Corporation (9.8%), and CNH Industrial (7.99%), driven by factors ranging from leadership changes to tariff-related supply chain concerns.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 14.09% following JP Morgan analyst downgrade with price target reduction from $19 to $16, maintaining Neutral rating
Stock fell 10.23% due to worse-than-expected Q1 financial results and announcement of smelter site sale to NYDIG
Stock slumped 13.93% with no specific catalyst mentioned, indicating broad sector weakness
Stock slipped 8.22%, likely in response to Amazon's acquisition of competitor Globalstar
Stock fell 5.94% following announcement of $6.4 billion acquisition of SECURE Waste Infrastructure
Stock slumped 10.86% amid broad market selling pressure in energy sector
Stock decreased 8.56% as part of broader energy sector weakness
Stock fell 9.8% amid broad selling pressure in energy sector
Stock slipped 11.08% following leadership transition announcement with CEO Jim Fitterling becoming executive chair and COO Karen Carter taking over as CEO
Stock decreased 7.99% as manufacturing-related shares traded lower due to concerns about $166 billion tariff refund delays affecting supply-chain dependent companies

title: Markets Shatter Records As Iran Opens Strait of Hormuz: This Week On Wall Street
author: Piero Cingari
description: Following Iran's agreement to open the Strait of Hormuz and cease using it as a weapon, markets surged to historic records. Oil prices plunged 15% to $80/barrel, the S&P 500 crossed 7,100 for the first time, and the Nasdaq 100 extended a 13-day winning streak. Energy stocks fell sharply while growth and crypto stocks rallied strongly on reduced inflation concerns.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Russell 2000 hit record 2,780, up 15% over 13 sessions with green closes every day of the week as small caps participated in broad market rally.
Energy sector stock fell ~10% on the week as crude oil prices collapsed 15% following the Strait of Hormuz opening, eliminating the war premium that had supported energy prices in Q1.
Energy stock declined ~10% as oil prices plunged 27% over two weeks with the resolution of Middle East tensions and removal of supply shock concerns.
Energy sector stock fell ~10% due to crude oil's sharp decline following the geopolitical de-escalation and opening of the Strait of Hormuz.
Surged 32% as broad risk appetite returned with reduced inflation fears and market euphoria from historic record-breaking week.
Rose 30% in its best week since 1999, benefiting from the return of risk appetite and growth stock rally following geopolitical resolution.
Rose 30% in its best week since 1999, benefiting from the return of risk appetite and growth stock rally following geopolitical resolution.
Added 25% as crypto names rallied alongside growth stocks on reduced inflation concerns and improved market sentiment.
Rallied 7.7% in its best weekly performance since October 2025 as automakers recovered on lower oil prices and improved economic outlook.
Gained 7% for its third consecutive week of gains, benefiting from lower energy costs and improved consumer sentiment.
Gained 7% for its third consecutive week of gains, benefiting from lower energy costs and improved consumer sentiment.

Gained 7% for its third consecutive week of gains, benefiting from lower energy costs and improved consumer sentiment.

Nasdaq 100 reached 26,700 with 13 consecutive winning sessions, its strongest streak since April 2020, driven by growth and tech stock rallies.

title: Oil Above $90, Pump Above $4 — And 7 Energy Stocks Still Trading At A Wide Discount
author: Piero Cingari
description: Seven major energy stocks are trading at historically low valuations (7x-11x forward P/E) despite oil prices above $90/barrel due to the Strait of Hormuz crisis. The sector has underperformed crude oil gains, creating a potential opportunity if the supply disruption persists, though risks remain if a ceasefire rapidly brings prices back down to $65-70.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Low-breakeven producer positioned to benefit from sustained $90+ oil prices, with 9.5x forward P/E and 12.2% analyst upside potential.
Low-breakeven producer trading at 11.3x forward P/E with 13.9% analyst upside, positioned to capture free cash flow from sustained high oil prices.
Trading at the lowest forward P/E (7.2x) among large-cap producers with 49.6% YTD gains, representing significant undervaluation relative to oil price strength and supply disruption environment.
Trading at 8.6x forward earnings with median analyst target implying 31.6% upside, showing the most analyst conviction despite already strong performance.
Carries widest analyst upside at 38.5% despite being the only name still negative YTD, suggesting significant recovery potential if supply concerns persist.
Refiner positioned to benefit from elevated crack spreads (margin between crude and refined products) with 10.7x forward P/E and 37.1% YTD gains.
Refiner with 11.2x forward P/E benefiting from widened crack spreads in elevated crude environment, with 44.4% YTD gains.
Up 27% YTD but underperformed crude oil's 38% gain, indicating sector-wide undervaluation relative to commodity strength; represents both opportunity and risk depending on Hormuz resolution.
